text: Let's visualize SGD with and without weight decay, to see how L2 regularization pulls weights toward zero.
We have a 2D weight space with axes w1 and w2. The blue contour lines show the loss landscape, forming an elliptical bowl. The yellow dot marks the minimum of the loss function.
Starting from the white dot, watch SGD without weight decay. The green path shows the optimizer converging directly toward the loss minimum.
Now with weight decay. The red path also moves toward lower loss, but notice how the trajectory bends toward the origin. The weight decay term adds a penalty proportional to the weight magnitude.

The update rule with weight decay includes a lambda times w term. This creates an additional force pulling the weights toward zero, which is why the final weights are closer to the origin than without regularization.
L2 regularization, implemented as weight decay, effectively pulls weights toward zero, preventing overfitting by discouraging large weight values.
